text.title: Backlog Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Backlog Management. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Backlog has ≤3 states (e.g. Now / Next / Icebox). More states = more drift.
rule.rationale: Backlog has ≤3 states (e.g. Now / Next / Icebox). More states = more drift.

rule.statement: Each state has an age cap; items beyond cap auto-flag for grooming.
rule.rationale: Each state has an age cap; items beyond cap auto-flag for grooming.

rule.statement: Weekly grooming ≤60 min; closes / merges / demotes drift.
rule.rationale: Weekly grooming ≤60 min; closes / merges / demotes drift.

rule.statement: Backlog items ÷ weekly throughput ≤ 3×; above = wishlist not plan.
rule.rationale: Backlog items ÷ weekly throughput ≤ 3×; above = wishlist not plan.

rule.statement: One backlog tool only; cross-system duplication kills truth.
rule.rationale: One backlog tool only; cross-system duplication kills truth.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-backlog-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
